picks.rationale: Cheapest priced offering at $29 vs $32 peers; supplier-e chats show no banana-related shorts or quality issues in the recent window.
Supplier-n is cheapest at $50/25lb (vs $56-62 peers) with no Brussels-sprouts-related issues in recent chats.
Supplier-b is the cheapest 50 lb pack-size match at $35 (vs $38 peers), with consistent recent deliveries of 50 lb carrots at the same price and no shorts or quality issues in chats.
Tied for cheapest at $48; supplier-o just confirmed and fulfilled an identical 4-case Celery Stalks 24ct order at $48 on Apr 23 with no shorts or quality issues, making them the most proven option.

Supplier-g and supplier-l both offer 24ct cilantro at $28, but supplier-l recently rejected a 24ct cilantro request (only had 60ct), so supplier-g is the more reliable pick at the matching pack size and price.

Supplier-q's English Cucumber MED 12ct at $12 is an exact match for the 'Cucumber Medium' 12-piece pack; cheaper offerings are different specs (Small, #2 grade, Mini) and supplier-q chats show no recent issues.
Supplier-h offers Large Cukes at $14 (cheapest 'Large' match) with clean recent chats, while supplier-p ($15) was just short on cukes on Apr 22.

Only priced offering for grape tomatoes; supplier-f has been reliably delivering grape cherry 12x1lb in recent days with no shorts or quality issues on this SKU (the Apr 17 mush complaint was on a different item, 5x6 vine ripe).
Supplier-n is cheapest at $34 with a matching 48-bunch pack size, has been a consistent green onion supplier in recent chats with no quality issues, and was already quoted at $34 for Apr 23.
supplier-k's 'Green Jumbo' offering is the exact spec match at $55 and recent chats show no issues on green peppers.

Supplier-l ties for cheapest at $35 with the closest pack size to 113ct (100ct vs supplier-n's 75ct), and recent chats show they routinely fill our 113-piece lemon line at $34-36 with no active shorts or quality issues.

picks.raw_row_text: ONION RED JUMBO BAG BLUE BABY 25 LB 20.00
picks.rationale: Only offering that matches red onion in a 25 lb pack; price is reasonable and recent chats show no red-onion shorts or quality issues with supplier-p.

picks.raw_row_text: ON005 LARGE 50 LB BAG 20.00 * 19.00 * COOKING ONIONS
picks.rationale: Supplier-n has been our consistent source for Onion Cooking Medium 50 lb at $19 across recent orders (Apr 14/15/17/21/24) with no shorts or quality issues, and is the cheapest 50 lb cooking onion option.

supplier-l is the only supplier offering orange peppers (XL Prime Time) at a stable $20 over the last two days, and recent chats show no quality or short issues; PEPPERS commodity had no orange options.
Cheapest red pepper offering at $30 (XXL grade) from a reliable supplier with no recent shorts or quality issues; no 25lb pack exists in the market today, so 5kg/11lb pack is the standard substitute.
Supplier-L's $12 Sweet Darling is well below peers ($18, $32, $32) and chat history shows consistent strawberry supply at $6-$8 with no quality issues, so the low price is normal for them.

Cheapest 5x6 lister supplier-n is shorted ('No tomatoes' on Apr 21); supplier-f at $110 is the next cheapest and team has been actively buying 5x6 from them (earlier quality concern was explained as a variety mismatch and resolved).
Supplier-e is the only offering matching the exact item (Hot House) and pack size (15 lb), with no quality or short issues in recent chats.

Cheapest local 5lb brown/portobello cap match (Unionville); recent chat confirms 'Mushroom Brown Caps Local 5 lb $15.50' was bought at this exact price with no quality issues, while white-mushroom rows aren't portobello and other brown options are pricier or wrong pack.
Only supplier offering the exact 3 lb Heritage Blend pack at $12; consistent supply history with supplier-m for this item (one isolated short on Apr 17, but orders since have been fine).
Supplier-p offers a matching 60ct pack at $56, $2 cheaper than the only other 60ct option (supplier-q at $58), with no cilantro-related shorts or complaints in recent chats.

Supplier-n offers Ginger Root in the requested 30 lb carton at $24 — cheapest at that pack size, recently ordered without issue, and already on the Apr 23 order with no shorts or quality complaints in chats.

Supplier-f's offering sheet only captured a 5x6 row, but recent chats (Apr 21, 22, 23) show them actively delivering Tomato 6x7 25 lb at $90/case (Crimson RGR variety) — cheaper than any field-tomato alternative; supplier-n said 'No tomatoes' on Apr 21, and the prior quality issue was on 5x6 Vine Ripe, not 6x7.
supplier-f is cheapest at $40 for Broccoli Crowns and recent chats show no broccoli quality issues or shorts.
Sole priced offering at a stable $48/24ct, and recent supplier-m chats show no shorts or quality issues on green leaf lettuce.

Supplier-i's 3X1LB pack matches the 3 lb pack size exactly, is cheaper than supplier-m's 8x5oz alternative, and recent chats show no quality or short issues on Spring Mix Mesclun (ordered routinely on Apr 21 and Apr 23).
Supplier-g is the only offering in the ROMAINE HEARTS bucket and is the regular supplier of this exact item (Romaine Heart 6 Pack, 7 packs) with no quality issues in recent chats.
Only priced offering for Roma tomatoes; recent supplier chats show the mush quality issue was on 5x6 vine ripe (different variety), not Roma, so no active blocker.
Cheapest 9-count offering at $28 vs $30-$36 from other 9ct suppliers; recent chats show consistent $28 cantaloupe pricing with no shorts or quality issues on this item.
Cheapest Spanish onion offering at $30 (Super Colossal is a like-or-better grade vs Jumbo, Spanish onions ship in standard 50 lb sacks); supplier-f chats show no onion issues.
Cheapest brown 5lb match; supplier-a/c are white (wrong variety) and supplier-l confirmed this exact item at $15.50 in recent chats with no quality issues.

picks.raw_row_text: Mushroom White - Choice - 5 lb per case - $11.00
picks.rationale: Supplier-a is cheapest at $11 vs next white 5lb at $13.50, with consistent daily deliveries of this exact item and no recent shorts or quality issues in chats.
Supplier-k is the cheapest priced offering and recent chats show they have consistently supplied size-6 pineapples on Apr 14/16/21 with no quality issues.
Supplier-c is the cheapest priced offering and chats confirm an already-placed Apr 23 order of 4x Melon Honeydew Jumbo 5pc with no quality issues.
supplier-k carries the matching 4lb (2x2lb) baby spinach pack and Jitto has been ordering Spinach Baby 2 x 2 lb from them weekly without quality issues; no other supplier offers this exact pack.
supplier-d-pt1 is the cheapest Red Delicious offering at $52/80ct ($0.65/piece) vs supplier-q's Red Del 72ct at $56 ($0.78/piece), with no chat issues on file.

Only priced 4x5lb coleslaw offering; recent quality issue was credited and supplier moved to new growing area, team continued buying 7 cases on Apr 21 at $26 so issue is acknowledged/managed, not active unresolved.
Supplier-k is the only offering that matches the requested 6x3 lb peeled garlic pack size, with no issues in recent chats (cheaper rows are different pack sizes or paste).
Supplier-i is our regular mint supplier (recurring 12-bunch orders billed cleanly Apr 14/15/16/23 with no quality issues) and offers the lowest priced row at $6.
Supplier-h offers heirloom 10 lb at $35, $1 cheaper than supplier-j's $36, and recent chats show clean fulfillment with an Apr 24 heirloom order already placed at the same price.

Supplier-b is the cheapest red 16-head option at $25 vs $28-$34 from peers, and chats confirm they've reliably delivered this exact line including a confirmed Apr 23 order with no shorts or quality issues.
supplier-p is the only offering that exactly matches Fine French beans in the 14x400g pack size; cheaper options are different specs (bulk green beans, wirebound crates) and not interchangeable.
Supplier-p is cheapest at $24 with an exact 113ct match for juicing oranges, and recent chats show consistent successful deliveries of this exact item with no shorts.

Cheapest true 24ct match (supplier-i $26 is a different pack: 8x113g crispy hearts); we just bought the same Andy Boy 24-piece romaine from supplier-g on Apr 21 at $43 with no quality issues, so $45 is the best $/quality fit.
Supplier-e's $28 lime pack matches the 54-piece spec exactly (chats confirm '5 Lime 54 pieces $28.00' billed Apr 23); other suppliers only offer 175ct cartons.
Supplier-i offers the exact 10 lb pack at $13 — cheapest in absolute terms, matches pack size, and chats show this line has been delivered reliably with no shorts or quality issues.
Cheapest priced offering and supplier-i has a clean recent track record on curly parsley (delivered 12x40g at $12.25 on Apr 22/23 with no shorts or complaints).

Supplier-l is tied for cheapest at $35 and recent chats show they regularly fulfill the 88ct lemon line with no quality issues or shorts; closest size match in the cheapest tier (supplier-n's $35 alternative is only 75ct).
Only listing matching both Navel variety and the 50-piece pack spec (48-72 bracket); chats show no recent issues with supplier-k on oranges.
Supplier-q is the cheapest priced option ($68 vs $85 at supplier-d-pt2) with no tomatillo-related issues in recent chats.
Supplier-n is the only offering matching the 12x3 (12-pack) carton spec at $50, with no unresolved issues in recent chats.

Supplier-i is cheaper ($11.75 vs $12.00) and recent chats confirm thyme has been delivered reliably with no shorts or quality issues.

Best match for 'Medium Choice' spec — medium-grade Canadian cucumber at $12 (cheaper alternatives are #2/utility grade or small/mini packs, and supplier-q chats show no recent quality or short issues).
